items.title: 20 years after Katrina, what’s next for New Orleans?#shorts #katrina #nola #business
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>How did New Orleans come back 20 years after the city’s worst disaster? We speak to Tulane's Walter Isaacson on "Wall Street Week" https://bloom.bg/46EnYbz</article>

items.title: Mexico’s Nearshoring Boom Faces Trade Risks
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>For decades, US companies sent production to China, but now many are moving factories to Mexico. Last year, we explored the rise of nearshoring, the risks facing investors, and how US trade politics could reshape North America’s supply chain. A year later, President Donald Trump’s tariffs are upsetting the dynamics of North American auto manufacturing, dealing a blow to Canadian auto parts suppliers.
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

items.title: Is AI Taking Away Tech Jobs?
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Sarah Franklin, CEO of HR platform Lattice, says the impact of artificial intelligence is already being seen in the tech jobs market. She joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech."</article>

items.title: The F1 Surge: McLaren Racing Valued at $4.1B
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>The team leading the F1 championship isn’t just winning on the track — McLaren Racing just clocked a $4.1 billion valuation in a new stake sale. It’s the latest sign investors can’t get enough of the sport’s momentum.  McLaren Racing CEO Zak Brown joined Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about the momentum.
--------

items.title: Botswana Seeks De Beers Control; Jumia Rides Trade War Tailwind | Bloomberg Next Africa
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>On this episode of Next Africa: Botswana President Duma Boko tells Bloomberg Television he aims to complete a deal to take a majority stake in De Beers by the end of next month. Botswana wants control of the diamond company to exercise greater sway over the entire international supply chain, particularly to emphasize the superiority of natural diamonds. Plus, as US President Donald Trump's tariff war redraws global trade maps, we explore how auto makers from the Global South are challenging the dominance of Western players on the continent. We also hear from Jumia CEO, Francis Dufay, who says the current trade environment is causing a shift in global supply chains -- making it easier to bring goods from China to Africa. And Ethiopia switched on Africa's largest hydropower project -- a milestone that's been a source of tension with Egypt and Sudan over river Nile's flow.

00:00 - Introduction
00:58 - Ethiopia powers up Africa's largest dam  
07:28 - Investing Africa: Richard Okello, Sango Capital
08:39 - Botswana President on diamond demand downturn
15:03 - US tariffs hit Africa auto sector
18:51 - Jumia CEO on supply shift from China  
--------

items.title: Trump Says Government Will Shut Down if it Has To | Balance of Power 9/26/2025
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Republican Representative of Arkansas, French Hill, talks about the impending government shutdown and what needs to be done to avoid it, Former US Attorney, Nick Akerman, talks about the legitimacy of the case against Former FBI Director James Comey, and Former Democratic Representative of California, Jane Harman, discusses the ongoing conflicts in Gaza and Ukraine.

items.title: How New Orleans Rebuilt After Katrina and What Comes Next
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>On the twentieth anniversary of Hurricane Katrina we go to New Orleans to see how residents and businesses came together to rebuild a city that was nearly destroyed. From Antoine’s in the French Quarter to the Lower Ninth Ward, we hear stories of survival, resilience and the work that still lies ahead.
--------

items.title: Jollibee Takes on Colonel Sanders in a Battle for US Palates
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Filipino fast food giant Jollibee is aiming to triple its North American presence by opening 350 stores across the US. But will banana ketchup spaghetti and ube pies be enough to win over American taste buds? Kristine Aquino explains.  https://t.co/z5x9RpvgxL</article>

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are KPMG’s Diane Swonk, Ameriprise’s Anthony Saglimbene, Oppenheimer’s Brian Nagel, ABC’s Robert Herjavec, MNTN’s Mark Douglas, Interactive Brokers’ Steve Sosnick, S&P Global Ratings’ Nora Wittstruck, George Mason University’s Thomas Hoenig, Pro Shop’s Chad Mumm, Grammy-Nominated Music Producer Steve Aoki.

items.title: Test of Time | North America Auto Tariffs, American Worker Shortage, 20 Years After Katrina
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>This week, we revisit two stories we brought you over the past year and discuss what has changed since then. How has trade between the US and Mexico shifted since President Donald Trump took office, and where do Canadian auto parts fit into the trade spat between the North American countries? And, how one manufacturing plant in Fargo, North Dakota is navigating legal immigration challenges that impact its workforce. Later, how did New Orleans come back twenty years after the city’s worst disaster?

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Canadian Autos in Trade Fight
00:13:54 - American Worker Shortage
00:23:49 - New Orleans after Katrina
00:37:26 - What's Next for New Orleans

items.title: What’s behind the White House’s Argentina rescue plan? #politics #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>President Trump and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent say they’re planning broad financial support for Argentina. But why now? And how would it work? @davidgura and @Pat_Gillespie break it down on the Big Take podcast. https://trib.al/QPQwd5Y</article>

items.title: Netanyahu Slams Western Countries Recognizing Palestinian State #politics
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu struck a defiant tone in his speech to the United Nations General Assembly, vowing to continue his country’s war in Gaza until Hamas is destroyed and slamming Western nations that recognized Palestinian statehood.</article>

items.title: Consumer Spending Gains, Comey Charged | Bloomberg Businessweek Daily 9/26/2025
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>On today's episode of Bloomberg Businessweek Daily, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec discuss the strong posting of consumer spending despite sticky inflation. Nerdwallet Senior Economist Elizabeth Renter and Bloomberg's Michael McKee join to break down the PCE print. Also on today's show, 1-800-Flowers Chief Executive Officer Adolfo Villagomez talks consumer demand, Bloomberg's Chris Strohm on former FBI Director James Comey facing charges, and MONT y MER Chief Executive Officer Brendan Kennedy discusses EMS technology enhancing workouts.

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Bloomberg Businessweek Daily Starts
00:05:49 - NerdWallet’s Elizabeth Renter and Michael McKee Break Down Consumer Spending Posting Strong Gain Despite Sticky Inflation
00:13:29 - 1-800-Flowers CEO Adolfo Villagomez Talks Consumer Demand, Retail Expansion, Growth
00:20:46 - Chris Strohm on Fmr. FBI Director Comey Facing Down Trump DOJ Following Criminal Indictment
00:26:08 - Max Abelson Breaks Down Epstein’s Private Emails Showing Support, Advice from Elite Network
00:34:31 - MONT y MER CEO Brendan Kennedy and Chris Rovzar Discuss EMS Technology Like Katalyst Suits Enhancing Workouts
00:46:14 - Alexandra Semenova with Stocks on the Move
